workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just got this from Karen Berg at Iron Creek Mining -- I need help figuring out escalation triage. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 14:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining escalation triage

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 12:39 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 17:15 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Iron Creek Mining

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-Q trap (No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-Q failure mode (No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-Q -- No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
